reasoning:
1. The 360-day threshold (~31.17 riots per month) encompasses the massive August 2024 "EndBadGovernance" national protest. Riot event distributions heavily skew right, meaning a huge outlier month drags the annual mean well above the median. Normal months will therefore typically fall below this inflated average.
2. 2025-06-01: May 2025 has seen no major protracted riots reported (April 7 protests were swiftly dispersed). With the 30-day window nearly complete, reaching the threshold is highly unlikely. 
3. 2025-06-24: Near-term, with no known massive catalysts for June. Uncertainty increases slightly, keeping probability low but somewhat higher than early June.
4. 2025-08-23: The 30-day window (July 24 - Aug 22) covers the one-year anniversary of the August 1, 2024, protests. Protest movements frequently rekindle around such anniversaries, notably elevating the risk of a new spike in riots.
5. 2025-11-21: The furthest forecast horizon. In alignment with increasing ignorance for distant non-specific periods, uncertainty progresses toward 0.5, reflecting the persistent latent risk of sudden economic or political flare-ups in Nigeria.
